data.title: Tandem Diabetes reported $240.678 million in sales, missing earnings with a loss of 78 cents per share. Stocks dropped due to the dull outlook.
data.description: Tandem Diabetes Care Inc. saw a decline in stock value after revising its annual guidance. The diabetes company reported an adjusted loss of 78 cents per share, missing the expected loss of 40 cents. However, they reported sales of $240.678 million, exceeding expectations of $238.57 million, showing an increase of 8% year over year. Shipments in the United States totaled around 21,000 pumps, while international shipments were about 9,000 pumps. Tandem narrowed its fiscal 2025 sales guidance to $1 billion from the earlier range of $997 million-$1.01 billion, compared to the consensus of $1.004 billion. These adjustments were made based on insights gained in the first half of the year. Leigh Vosseller, the CFO, highlighted expectations for U.S. growth, pharmacy channel initiatives, and international sales.

data.title: "Global White Hydrogen Market to reach $56.8 million by 2030, growing at 7.1% CAGR. Naturally occurring and cost-effective clean energy alternative."
data.description: The global white hydrogen market is projected to reach $56.8 million by 2030, with a growth rate of 7.1% between 2025-2030. This market study by BCC Research delves into the sources of white hydrogen and its potential as a clean energy alternative. White hydrogen, derived largely from natural sources like ultramafic rocks and volcanic activity, is gaining attention for its cost-effectiveness and ease of extraction compared to green or blue hydrogen. Technological advancements in exploration tools are facilitating the discovery and extraction of white hydrogen, reducing risks and costs. This report is crucial in the context of ongoing efforts to reduce carbon emissions globally. The urgent need for sustainable energy solutions is driving interest in white hydrogen, prompting increased exploration and research efforts to leverage its potential.

data.title: Eli Lilly Co. reported strong Q2 2025 earnings with revenue growing 38% yoy, EPS increasing by 61% to $6.31, and revenue guidance raised to $60-62 billion for the year. Strategic initiatives include new drug launches, clinical trials, and global expansions. Collaboration with the administration for drug pricing reforms being discussed.
data.description: Eli Lilly Co. reported its second-quarter 2025 financial results, with revenue growing by 38% compared to Q2 2024, reaching $5.2 billion. The company shared top-line clinical data from the ATTAIN 1 trial, showing patients lost 27 pounds on orforglipron. They also highlighted key milestones like FDA approval for new dosing for Kisandla and positive results in various Phase 3 trials. The company distributed $1.3 billion in dividends, executed $700 million in share repurchases, and raised revenue guidance to $60-62 billion for 2025. Non-GAAP earnings per share increased by 61% to $6.31, with strong performances from key products like Mounjaro and Sepban. Lilly also discussed challenges in the US pharmaceutical market and their commitment to reducing patient costs and enhancing access to medicines. They are aiming for future growth by increasing R&D investment and production capacity, including plans for new manufacturing facilities in the US. Overall, the company's financial performance in the first half of 2025 was robust, leading to increased revenue and earnings per share guidance for the year.

data.title: Hyatt Hotels stock rises as Q2 earnings beat estimates: EPS 68 cents (vs. 65 cents), sales $1.81B (vs. $1.73B). RevPAR up 1.6%. Net room growth 11.8%. Gross fees $301M. Adjusted EBITDA $303M.
data.description: Hyatt Hotels Corporation (NYSE: H) shares experienced a rise in trading on Thursday following the release of their second-quarter financial results. The company reported adjusted earnings per share of 68 cents, surpassing the analyst consensus of 65 cents. Quarterly sales reached $1.81 billion, exceeding the expected $1.73 billion. Comparable system-wide hotel revenue per available room (RevPAR) saw a 1.6% increase compared to the same quarter in 2024. Net rooms also saw a notable growth of 11.8% year over year, or 6.5% without factoring in acquisitions. Gross fees amounted to $301 million, marking a 9.5% increase from the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA for the quarter totaled $303 million, a 1.1% decrease year over year but reflecting a 9.0% increase on a pro forma basis. These details and more can be found in the complete article on benzinga.com.

data.description: NUVA utilizes the Provenance Blockchain to provide institutional-grade tokenized assets, including stablecoin securities and Home Equity Line of Credits (HELOCs).

data.title: Radiance Solar appoints Todd Shutley as COO, with past roles at Truist Bank. Shutley's experience positions him well for his new role.
data.description: Radiance Solar, a prominent solar EPC company, has appointed Todd Shutley as their new COO. Shutley, who previously served as COO at Inlight Real Estate Partners and held various senior roles at Truist Bank, brings a wealth of operational leadership experience to his new role. This move highlights Radiance Solar's commitment to strengthening its executive team. Todd Shutley's expertise will play a key role in the company's strategy going forward.

data.title: Emera Inc. converts 1,300 out of 4,866,814 Series A Shares into Series B Shares; shareholders notified.
data.description: Emera Incorporated in Halifax, Nova Scotia, has informed shareholders that out of its total 4,866,814 issued Series A Shares, 1,300 have been tendered for conversion into Series B Shares on a one-for-one basis. This announcement pertains to the Cumulative 5-Year Rate Reset First Preferred Shares, Series A, and the Cumulative Floating Rate First Preferred Shares, Series B. Emera, listed on both TSX and NYSE under the ticker symbol EMA, has taken this action as part of a strategic move within the company.

data.title: Nebius stock surges 20% after Q2 results show huge revenue jump, with raised guidance._NVDA-backed cloud provider.
data.description: The stock of Nebius surged by almost 20% following a significant increase in revenue reported by the cloud provider, backed by Nvidia. In the second quarter, Nebius Group saw a substantial boost in its financial performance. This positive development led to a surge in its stock price, reflecting investor confidence. The company also revised its guidance upwards, indicating strong prospects for future growth.

data.title: Firefly Aerospace ups IPO; debut on Thursday. Rocket Lab, Kratos Defense down pre-earnings.
data.description: Firefly Aerospace is set to make its debut on Thursday with an increased initial public offering (IPO). Rocket Lab and Kratos Defense are both experiencing declines in anticipation of their upcoming earnings reports. Firefly Aerospace's IPO has been upsized, creating buzz in the market. Rocket Lab is seeing a slight ease in its performance, while investors eagerly await its financial results. The latest developments point to a dynamic and competitive environment in the aerospace industry.

data.title: Billionaire Robert Rosenkranz finds wisdom in ancient Stoics; seeks to apply in modern life.
data.description: Robert Rosenkranz, a billionaire known for his unique perspective, draws inspiration from the ancient Stoics for guidance in his life. Despite his wealth and success, Rosenkranz's philosophy is rooted in principles that he believes offer timeless wisdom. His approach contrasts with the traditional views of many in his position, making him stand out in the business world. The influence of Stoic teachings on Rosenkranz's decisions and mindset reflects his commitment to finding innovative and meaningful ways to navigate contemporary challenges.

data.title: Fortinet's Q2 results meet expectations, but stock falls; confusion over firewall refresh. Cybersecurity sector sells off.
data.description: Fortinet's stock saw a surge after their Q2 earnings report, with revenue and billings coming in slightly above analyst estimates. However, the June quarter guidance was in line with expectations. Despite this positive news, the stock fell on confusion among investors regarding the firewall refresh. This led to a sell-off in cybersecurity stocks.

data.title: Intel stock falls after Trump says CEO Lip-Bu Tan "must resign" due to conflict of interest.
data.description: Intel's shares experienced a decline following President Trump's statement that CEO Lip-Bu Tan "must resign," citing him as "highly conflicted." The stock saw a drop in response to this development.

data.title: Caterpillar stock downgrade drags Dow; Nasdaq maintains gains. Dow Jones impacted, Nasdaq holds steady.
data.description: The stock of Caterpillar experienced a decline following a downgrade, which had a negative impact on the Dow Jones Industrial Average. The Dow was affected by this drop, while the Nasdaq managed to maintain a positive gain. The development was covered in a live broadcast titled "Stock Market Today: Dow Weighed Down By Caterpillar; Nasdaq Holds A Gain, For Now."

data.title: Apple stock rises 3% with $100B investment. Eli Lilly crashes 14% due to drug trial results which caused $100B loss. Market dips overall.
data.description: U.S. equities experienced fluctuations due to corporate earnings and new tariff announcements by President Trump. The S&P 500 fell by 0.3% to 6,328, the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropped by 0.8% to 43,865, and the Nasdaq 100 remained nearly unchanged at 23,307. Apple Inc. (AAPL) saw a 3% increase after gaining 5.1% the previous day, with investors optimistic about the company avoiding new import duties. Apple committed to investing an additional $100 billion in U.S. manufacturing. Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) had a significant downturn of 14%, attributed to its experimental weight-loss pill, which yielded a 12.4% weight loss, below analyst expectations. This led to a market capitalization loss of over $100 billion. More details on this news available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Peloton beats Q4 EPS estimate at 5 cents, revenue at $606.90 million, down 6% YoY. Stock falls. Paid subscriptions at 2.80M, down 6% YoY.
data.description: Peloton Interactive (NASDAQ: PTON) released its quarterly earnings report on Thursday. In the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, the company posted earnings per share of $0.05, surpassing the analyst consensus of a $0.06 loss. Despite this, PTON stock is currently experiencing a decline. The fitness-equipment maker achieved quarterly sales of $606.90 million, which is a 6% decrease from the previous year but exceeds the expected $580.24 million. Furthermore, the Connected Fitness Products Revenue dropped by $13.5 million, a 6% decline year-over-year due to reduced sales and deliveries offset by a shift towards higher-priced products. The Ending Paid Connected Fitness Subscriptions for the quarter were reported at 2.80 million, reflecting a 6% decrease year-over-year. Subscription Revenue saw a decrease of $23.2 million.
